items.title: The rising cost of capital for companies today is starting to spook the stock market: ‘The worry is the spending might not pay off’
items.description: Alphabet says it plans to throw even more money at the AI build-out. The Iran war, oil back at $100 a barrel and rising bond yields make it all the more difficult for the market to stomach.

items.title: The Treasury market is flashing a warning sign for home buyers. Are 7% mortgage rates next?
items.description: The 30-year fixed-rate mortgage edged up to its highest level of 2026.

items.title: The super rich use 401(k)s and IRAs to sidestep taxes on millions of dollars. This proposed law would cut them off.
items.description: More than 200 individuals had a total of more than $85 billion in tax-sheltered retirement accounts

items.title: Most FDA advisors say the BPC-157 peptide should be made available in the U.S. Here’s what happens next.
items.description: The FDA is deciding whether seven peptides like BPC-157 and TB-500 can be legally compounded, potentially opening the door to broader use in Americans.

items.title: Global oil tops $100 after Houthis strike Saudi tankers and Trump threatens ‘military punishment’ on Iran
items.description: Oil futures climbed sharply Thursday, with global benchmark Brent crude topping $100 a barrel for the first time since May, after Yemen’s Houthi militants claimed attacks on two Saudi Arabian tankers

items.title: Are you looking for financial advice? This is the best way to ask for it.
items.description: Whether you’re emailing an expert or posting on a public forum, you need some ground rules

items.title: Lockheed Martin’s stock has best day in 25 years as push to build more missiles faster pays off
items.description: Lockheed Martin’s stock jumps as a ramp up in missile production helped lead to an earnings beat and raise.

items.title: ‘I have a $2,000 deductible’: I reported a cracked windshield on my Hertz rental car. Can they charge me?
items.description: “I never heard a rock hit the windshield.”

items.title: The August stock-market slump is a myth — so why does Wall Street keep repeating it?
items.description: More than 200 years of data show stocks typically gain in August, while market volatility sits well below average.

items.title: As defense spending surges, these stocks could make you the most money
items.description: Palantir tops a screen of defense companies for growth through 2028, but most of the expected best growers are small-cap stocks.
